Skip to dashboard content

The Agent Command Center

A read-only production dashboard for reviewing system status, safety boundaries, static schemas, and operator workflow readiness. Includes Original Phase 5A client-side operator workflow shell, Phase 5B request packet builder, Phase 5C review board, Phase 5D handoff composer, Phase 5E runbook simulator, Original +1 controlled automation readiness layer, and Original +1B operator console contract layer.

Production-hosted. Static/inert. No command execution. No deploy, merge, push, or mutation controls.

READ-ONLY DASHBOARD BACKEND ACTIONS DISABLED NO COMMAND EXECUTION NO DEPLOY CONTROLS NO MERGE CONTROLS NO SECRET ACCESS

Command Center Overview

Production Presentation & Safety Review

Review the core project roadmap, safety boundaries, static schema previews, readiness QA, and backend boundary blueprints. Technical audits and raw session data are available in the sections below.

Phase 1 status

PASS_WITH_HIGH_CONFIDENCE

Phase 1 backend remains the source of truth.

Phase 2 status

PASS_WITH_HIGH_CONFIDENCE

Phase 2 TUI and contracts remain intact.

Phase 3 status

PASS_WITH_HIGH_CONFIDENCE

Read-Only Operations Dashboard build and exports are available.

Safety status

WARNING

Production-hosted dashboard with deployment, merge, push, secret access, and command execution disabled.

Roadmap status

INFO

Ready for backend architecture blueprint review. Future backend integration remains a later phase.

Roadmap Re-Anchor

The inserted backend safety track is now locked and verified.

The project is returning to the original roadmap:

  • Original Phase 1 — CLI / Command Packet Layer (Complete)
  • Original Phase 2 — TUI / Terminal Operator Layer (Complete)
  • Original Phase 3 — Static Dashboard (Complete)
  • Original Phase 4 — Hosted / Production Dashboard Polish (ACTIVE)
  • Original Phase 5 — Interactive Operator Workflow Layer (Planned)
  • Original +1 — Controlled Agent / Automation Layer (Planned)
  • Original +1B — Operator Console Consolidation & Automation Contract Layer (ACTIVE)
  • Original +1C — Readiness Scoring, Contract QA & No-Go Decision Layer (ACTIVE)

Original Phase 4 — Hosted / Production Dashboard Polish remains the hosted dashboard baseline.

Current active direction: Original +1C — Readiness Scoring, Contract QA & No-Go Decision Layer

Note: Phase 4E is intentionally deferred.

Phase 4D Strategic Preview
Live auth false DISABLED
Database false DISABLED
Queue storage false DISABLED
Execution false DISABLED
External APIs false DISABLED

This packet is generated locally. It is not saved. It is not sent anywhere. It is not queued. It is not executed. It does not write to the backend. It does not mutate GitHub or Netlify. It disappears on refresh unless the operator copies it manually.

      Original +1 — Controlled Automation Readiness Layer
      

        


  

    CONTROLLED AUTOMATION READINESS
    

      READINESS ONLY — NO LIVE AUTOMATION — NO EXECUTION — NO MUTATION — NO BACKEND WRITES — NO DEPLOY / MERGE / PUSH / PR CONTROLS — FUTURE AUTH / STORAGE / APPROVAL REQUIRED
    

  

  

    Original +1 — Controlled Automation Readiness Layer. This control-room shell defines future automation boundaries, role gates, dry-run evidence, and rollback/no-go contracts without enabling real execution.
  


  

    

      

        
Automation Readiness Overview Panel

        READINESS ONLY
      

      
Select a future-facing action, role, and gate state to preview how the readiness layer classifies automation without turning it on.

Execution Boundary Panel

        NO LIVE ACTION
      

      
This boundary exists to show what is still impossible in the current build. It should remain inert until a future implementation phase adds the required backend, auth, and audit systems.

      

      

        
The current build cannot execute commands, mutate GitHub or Netlify, deploy, merge, push, create PRs, write backend data, store queues, or persist approvals.

      Original +1B — Operator Console Consolidation & Automation Contract Layer
      

        


  

    OPERATOR CONSOLE CONSOLIDATION
    
AUTOMATION CONTRACT LAYER

    
CONTRACTS ONLY — COPY/PASTE ONLY — READINESS ONLY — NO LIVE AUTOMATION — NO EXECUTION — NO MUTATION — NO BACKEND WRITES — NO DEPLOY / MERGE / PUSH / PR CONTROLS

  

  
Original +1B — Operator Console Consolidation & Automation Contract Layer. This layer consolidates the Phase 5A-5E workflow and the Original +1 readiness shell into one coherent operator console without enabling live automation.

Formal Automation Contract Schema Panel
SCHEMA PACK

Copy Output Hub Panel

Master Safety Boundary Panel
SAFETY

      
All outputs are local, temporary, and copy/paste only. Nothing is saved, submitted, queued, executed, or sent to any backend or external service.

      

      
Future real automation requires separate auth, storage, audit, queue, approval, and backend write systems that are not present yet.

      MVP-1 — Request Lifecycle Runtime + Persistence Scaffold
      

        


  

    MVP PRODUCT TRACK
    
REQUEST LIFECYCLE RUNTIME — PERSISTENCE ADAPTER SCAFFOLD — DATABASE MIGRATION SCAFFOLD — REAL PRODUCT PATH

    
STORAGE PROVIDER DECISION REQUIRED — AUTH PROVIDER DECISION REQUIRED

    
RUNTIME EXECUTION DISABLED — EXTERNAL MUTATION DISABLED — NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_AUTOMATION

Current recommendation

        
  • MVP_RUNTIME_SCAFFOLD_READY
  • PERSISTENCE_PROVIDER_DECISION_REQUIRED
  • REAL_AUTH_PROVIDER_DECISION_REQUIRED

Request Lifecycle Runtime Panel
FLOW

      
request_received → request_validated → storage_not_configured → dry_run_plan_generated → approval_required → audit_event_prepared → blocked_before_execution → ready_for_human_review

      

        
Lifecycle states

        
  • request_received
  • request_validated
  • storage_not_configured
  • dry_run_plan_generated
  • approval_required
  • audit_event_prepared
  • blocked_before_execution
  • ready_for_human_review

        
Forbidden states

        
  • executing
  • executed
  • deployed
  • merged
  • pushed
  • pr_created
  • external_system_mutated
  • automation_started

SCHEMA

      

        

          Runtime result schema
          
FieldRequirement

          
runtime_result_idrequired
request_idrequired
actor_idrequired
actor_rolerequired
received_atrequired
lifecycle_staterequired
validation_resultrequired
storage_resultrequired
dry_run_resultrequired
approval_requirementrequired
audit_summaryrequired
no_go_flagsrequired
blocked_operationsrequired
recommended_next_actionrequired
real_execution_enabledrequired
external_mutation_enabledrequired
persistence_enabledrequired

        

      

      

        
      

    


    

      
Persistence Adapter Strategy Panel
STRATEGY

      
Future provider choices are documented only. None are configured in this phase.

      

        

          Persistence adapter choices
          
AdapterRequired env varsProduction suitabilityRisk notesMigration requirementsLocal dev notesStatus

          
neon_postgresNEON_API_KEY, NEON_CONNECTION_STRINGfuture_candidateManaged database path requires a firm auth decision and migration discipline.; Do not connect during this scaffold phase.schema migration runner; request lifecycle tables; audit append durability; approval record durabilityDo not read env vars in this phase.; Keep the adapter as a design document only.NOT_CONFIGURED
supabase_postgresSUPABASE_CONNECTION_STRING, SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEYfuture_candidateStrong product fit for hosted persistence, but it must stay disconnected until provider decisions are made.; Do not wire browser-side credentials here.schema migration runner; request lifecycle tables; audit append durability; approval record durabilityNo env reads in this phase.; Keep the strategy read-only and declarative.NOT_CONFIGURED
sqlite_local_dev_onlyNonelocal_dev_onlyUseful for local iteration only.; Must not be treated as durable production persistence.local schema bootstrap; file-backed dev databaseSuitable for developer preview runs.; Keep it out of deployment flows.NOT_CONFIGURED
netlify_blobsNETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN, NETLIFY_SITE_IDfuture_candidateMay work for lightweight artifact persistence, but it is not configured in this phase.; Do not treat blob storage as a database replacement without a deliberate design review.blob layout contract; request lifecycle index contract; audit snapshot contractKeep the provider unconnected while the runtime scaffold is being validated.NOT_CONFIGURED
external_managed_postgresMANAGED_POSTGRES_CONNECTION_STRINGfuture_candidateUse only after auth and lifecycle durability decisions are locked in.; Do not connect any external managed database in this scaffold phase.schema migration runner; request lifecycle tables; audit append durability; approval record durability; dry-run result durabilityKeep this as a strategic option until the provider decision is made.NOT_CONFIGURED

Product Gap Panel
GAPS

  • real auth provider
  • durable database
  • migrations applied
  • server-side request create/list/read endpoints
  • real audit append
  • real approval record persistence
  • real dry-run evidence persistence
  • first safe GitHub action after persistence/auth

      MVP-2 — Local Durable Request Persistence Runtime
      

        


  

    MVP-2
    
LOCAL DURABLE REQUEST PERSISTENCE — SQLITE LOCAL DEV ADAPTER — REQUEST REPOSITORY — LOCAL MIGRATION RUNNER

    
LIFECYCLE EVENT PERSISTENCE — PRODUCTION PERSISTENCE NOT CONFIGURED — REAL AUTH PROVIDER REQUIRED

    
LOCAL DEV ONLY — NO EXTERNAL MUTATION — NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_AUTOMATION

    
NEXT_STEP_CHOOSE_PRODUCTION_POSTGRES_AND_AUTH_PROVIDER

          Local persistence status
          
SettingValue

          
local SQLite persistence availabletrue
production persistence configuredfalse

current recommendationLOCAL_PERSISTENCE_READY_FOR_DEV_TESTING / PRODUCTION_PERSISTENCE_PROVIDER_REQUIRED / REAL_AUTH_PROVIDER_REQUIRED / NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_AUTOMATION / NEXT_STEP_CHOOSE_PRODUCTION_POSTGRES_AND_AUTH_PROVIDER

        

      

      

        
      

    


    

      
SQLite Adapter Panel
ADAPTER

      
The SQLite local dev adapter is the only durable persistence layer in this MVP-2 slice.

      
  • initialize_local_dev_database
  • create_request
  • get_request
  • list_requests
  • update_request_state
  • add_lifecycle_event
  • get_lifecycle_events
  • close

      

        
Local dev path

        
.agent_command_center/demo_runtime.sqlite3

      

      

        
      

    

  


  

    

      
Request Repository Panel
REPOSITORY

      
The repository wraps the adapter, validates request payloads, persists lifecycle states, and never executes automation.

      
  • wrap the SQLite adapter
  • validate request payloads before local storage
  • create and fetch requests
  • list requests
  • transition lifecycle state
  • record lifecycle events
  • never execute automation

      

        
      

    


    

      
Local Migration Runner Panel
MIGRATION

      
Migration runs are explicit, local-dev only, and never touch a production database.

      

        
Migration behavior

        
{
  "file_path": "14_backend/product_runtime/persistence/apply_local_migrations.py",
  "sql_path": "14_backend/product_runtime/migrations/001_mvp_request_lifecycle.sql",
  "apply_mode": "explicit local-dev only",
  "production_safe": true,
  "database_url_read": false,
  "env_reads": false,
  "external_network": false
}

      

      

        
      

    

  


  

    

      
Lifecycle Persistence Demo Panel
DEMO

      
The demo persists one safe planning request locally and advances it through the request lifecycle states.

      
  • request_received
  • request_validated
  • dry_run_plan_generated
  • approval_required
  • audit_event_prepared
  • blocked_before_execution
  • ready_for_human_review

      

        
Prepare safe deployment review packet

        
True local persistence, no external mutation, no automation.

      

      

        
      

    


    

      
Production Persistence Gap Panel
GAPS

      
The local SQLite path is ready for development, but production persistence still needs provider and auth decisions.

      
  • choose production Postgres provider
  • choose auth provider
  • add env/secrets later
  • add production migrations later
  • add real request API later
  • add server-side identity binding later

  • then_add_server_side_identity_binding

Env Contract Panel
ENV

      
Environment variable names are documented only. No values are shown.

      

        

          Supabase environment contract
          
Env namePurposeBrowser safeCurrent required

          
SUPABASE_PROJECT_REFProject metadata and project identity.PASSno
SUPABASE_URLProject URL for the Supabase project.PASSno
SUPABASE_ANON_KEYBrowser-safe public key for future authenticated reads.FAILno
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEYServer-only secret for admin and future write operations.PASSno
SUPABASE_DB_PASSWORDDatabase password used for CLI link/setup and direct access.PASSno
DATABASE_URLDirect Postgres connection string for future server-side database access.PASSno
MVP_ENABLE_SUPABASE_REQUEST_APIFeature gate for the request API boundary scaffold.PASSno
MVP_ENABLE_REQUEST_API_WRITESFeature gate for request API write paths.PASSno
MVP_ENABLE_SUPABASE_AUTHFeature gate for future Supabase Auth binding.PASSno

        

      

      

        
      

    

  


  

    

      
Request API Boundary Panel
API

      
The provider-status endpoint and request API boundary stay disabled until configuration is present.

      

        

          Request API boundary
          
EndpointPurposeBoundary state

          
GET /api/provider-statusRead-only Supabase provider statusread_only
GET /api/requestsRead-only request API boundary scaffolddisabled_by_default
POST /api/requestsWrite boundary scaffold with writes disabled by defaultwrites_disabled_by_default

        

      

      

        
Disabled unless configured

        
  • request API reads stay disabled by default
  • request API writes stay disabled by default
  • no Supabase network calls are executed in MVP-3

  "provider": "supabase",

    "SUPABASE_PROVIDER_SELECTED",

  }
}

      MVP-4 — Supabase Auth + RLS + Authenticated Request API
      

        


  

    MVP-4
    
SUPABASE AUTH POLICY — RLS POLICY SCAFFOLD — AUTHENTICATED REQUEST API

    
BEARER TOKEN REQUIRED — ANONYMOUS REQUESTS BLOCKED — SERVICE ROLE NEVER EXPOSED TO BROWSER

    
RLS REQUIRED BEFORE WRITES — REQUEST API REQUIRES AUTH — WRITES DISABLED UNTIL RLS REVIEW

    
NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_AUTOMATION — NEXT_STEP_APPLY_RLS_MIGRATION_AND_ENABLE_READS

  


  

    

      
Auth Policy Panel
AUTH

      

    

      auth policy
      bearer_jwt
      PASS
    

    
    

      RLS policy
      scaffold_only
      WARNING
    

    
    

      bearer required
      true
      PASS
    

    
    

      writes disabled
      false
      PASS
    

    

      

        
Bearer JWT and Authorization header required

        
auth.uid() binding required. ANONYMOUS REQUESTS BLOCKED

      

      

        
      

    


    

      
RLS Policy Panel
RLS

      
Deny-by-default RLS scaffold for request runtime tables. No public write policy is added.

      

        

          RLS policy model
          
PropertyValue

          
            
default policydeny_by_default

            
request owner policyauth.uid() owns request

            
role policyapp_roles controls elevated access

            
service role usageserver_admin_only

          
        

      

      

        
      

    

  


  

    

      
Request API Gate Panel
GATE

      
Authenticated request behavior stays scaffold-only until every gate is explicitly reviewed.

      

        

          Request API gates
          
GateRequired

          
provider configuredtrue
request API enabledtrue
auth enabledtrue
bearer token requiredtrue
writes enabled for POSTtrue
RLS review requiredtrue
anonymous access blockedtrue

        

      

      

        
No anonymous access. No writes by default. No service role exposure.

      MVP-5 — Supabase Migration Readiness + Authenticated Reads
      

        


  

    MVP-5
    
MIGRATION READINESS CHECK — MANUAL MIGRATION REVIEW REQUIRED — AUTHENTICATED REQUEST READS

    
READS REQUIRE BEARER TOKEN — ANON KEY + USER TOKEN ONLY — SERVICE ROLE NOT USED FOR READS

    
WRITES STILL DISABLED — RLS REVIEW REQUIRED — NO AUTOMATIC MIGRATION APPLY

    
NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_AUTOMATION — NEXT_STEP_MANUALLY_APPLY_MIGRATIONS_AND_ENABLE_AUTH_READS

      Supabase CLI required
      true

  • 001_supabase_request_runtime.sql
  • 002_supabase_auth_rls_policies.sql

SAFETY

  • verify RLS enable statements

Migration review checklist

        
  • confirm supabase cli is available and linked to the project ref
  • confirm readiness checker passes locally
  • review the migration SQL files
  • apply schema and RLS migrations only
  • keep request writes disabled

      

      

        
Authenticated reads checklist

        
  • confirm provider configured
  • confirm request API reads target is enabled
  • confirm auth target is enabled
  • confirm bearer token is required
  • confirm service role stays server-only

ANON KEY + USER BEARER TOKEN — RLS-ENFORCED REQUEST READS — SERVICE ROLE NOT USED

    
WRITES STILL DISABLED — POST WRITES BLOCKED — VERIFY WITH REAL USER TOKEN — NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_AUTOMATION

Real Reads Status Panel
IMPLEMENTED

      

        

          Real authenticated read state
          
PropertyValue

          
project refmobvzrkcsfbumgbwvkcp
read modereal_supabase_postgrest_get
auth validationsupabase_auth_user_endpoint
uses anon keytrue
uses user bearer tokentrue
uses service rolefalse
writes enabledfalse

Auth Token Validation Panel
AUTH

      
Tokens are validated against the Supabase Auth user endpoint: GET /auth/v1/user

      

        
Validation mechanism

        
    
          
  • Extraction from Authorization header
    • 
          
  • Server-side request to Supabase Auth
    • 
          
  • Anon key + user token binding
    • 
          
  • No service role involvement
    • 
        

      

      

        
      

    

  


  

    

      
Read Actions Panel
ACTIONS

      

        

          Implemented read actions
          
ActionState

          
        

      

      

        
      

    


    

      
Security Boundary Panel
SECURITY

      

        

          Read client helper functions
          
FunctionState

          
        

      

      

        
RLS enforcement

        
Row Level Security enforces user ownership

      

      

        
Write lock

        
All POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE methods are blocked

      

    

  


  

    

      
Smoke Test Panel
SMOKE

      
The smoke status endpoint /api/request-read-smoke-status allows safe verification of connectivity and auth.

      

        
Verification requirements

        
    
          
  • Real Supabase user token required for live test
    • 
          
  • Live read smoke test is optional for build pass
    • 
          
  • No tokens are stored in tracked files
    •

Workspace UI Status
READY

      
The operator workspace is implemented as a secure, token-aware interface for request management.

      

        
Authentication Mode

        
bearer token in memory only

      

      

        
      

    


    

      
Token Handling Panel
SECURITY

      
Bearer tokens are handled with zero-persistence security.

      
  • Token is in-memory only
  • No local-Storage usage
  • No session-Storage usage
  • No cook-ie usage
  • No Indexed-DB usage
  • Clear button required

    

  


  

    

      
API Client Panel
CLIENT

      
Browser API client configuration.

      
  • Calls Netlify Functions only
  • No direct Supabase calls
  • Authorization header only
  • No token logging

    


    

      
Create Form Panel
FORM

      
Controlled request creation interface.

      
  • Strict schema validation
  • Client-side UX validation
  • Server-side enforcement
  • Write flag gated

SECURITY

API

Payload Validation Panel
STRICT

      
  • required: reviewer_persona

CLIENT

      
  • auth: anon key + user bearer token only
  • service role: NOT USED

TEST

SAFETY

  • migration apply: PASS
  • automation: PASS

Pitchable Release Package
PACKAGE

      
The Agent Command Center is a read-only operator dashboard for review, synthesis, roadmap, and demo packaging. It explains the product, the operator flow, and the safety boundary without enabling live mutation.

      

        
        
        
      

    


    

      
Technical Architecture Summary
TECH

      
Static dashboard layer with exported markdown/json packets, read-only UI models, and no browser-side secret storage, direct Supabase access, or execution controls.

      

        
        
      

    

  


  

    

      
Release Capability Map
MAP

      
Authenticated request reads; feedback review; synthesis and product decisions; request draft conversion; roadmap prioritization; guided demo control room; pitchable release packaging.

      

        
        
      

    


    

      
Audience Variants
VERSIONS

      
    
        
  • Recruiter Version: concise product story and value map.
    • 
        
  • Founder Operator Version: workflow, safety, and operator leverage.
    • 
        
  • Technical Reviewer Version: architecture and safety boundary detail.
    • 
      

      

        
        
        
      

    

  




      

    

    

    

      MVP-31 — Demo Session Capture + External Review Feedback Loop
      

        


  

    MVP-31
    
PASS_WITH_MANUAL_SESSION_CAPTURE_AND_OPTIONAL_GATED_IMPORT

    
DEMO SESSION CAPTURE WORKSPACE — EXTERNAL REVIEW FEEDBACK LOOP

    
REVIEWER PERSONA SESSION — DEMO SESSION NOTES — FEEDBACK PACKET DRAFT

    
OPTIONAL FEEDBACK IMPORT GATED — TOKEN IN MEMORY ONLY — NO AUTOMATED OUTREACH

    
NO FAKE REVIEWER RESULTS — SERVICE ROLE NOT USED — UPDATE DELETE EXECUTE BLOCKED

    
AUTOMATION STILL DISABLED — NEXT_STEP_BUILD_RELEASE_REVIEW_METRICS_AND_SIGNAL_DASHBOARD — NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_AUTOMATION

  


  

    

      
Demo Session Setup Panel
SETUP

      
Choose the reviewer persona, demo goal, and product area before you capture the session.

      
      
      
      
      
      
      

        
      

    


    

      
Demo Session Notes Panel
NOTES

      
Capture objections, confusion points, praise, trust concerns, and the next step by hand.

      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
    

  


  

    

      
Feedback Packet Draft Panel
PACKET

      
The draft packet is built from the current session fields and only imported when the operator clicks the gated action.

      
{"reviewer_persona":"operator","demo_goal":"Pitch the release package and walk the review loop","product_area":"Pitchable release package + demo control room","substantive_feedback":"Fill in session notes to draft the packet."}

      

        
        
      

    


    

      
Optional Feedback Import Panel
GATED

      
Endpoint: /api/feedback?action=import. Token stays in memory only and manual submission is required.

      
      
      

        
        
        
      

      

        
      

      
No automated outreach, no email sending, and no browser persistence.

      
Awaiting explicit operator action.

    

  


  

    

      
Follow-Up Decision Panel
FOLLOW-UP

      
Keep the operator in control of the next step after the demo session is captured.

      
      
      
Review and refine internally before optional import.

      

        
      

    


    

      
Security Boundary Panel
SAFETY

      
The workspace stays manual, token-aware, and free of outreach automation.

      
    
        
  • No automated outreach.
    • 
        
  • No email sending.
    • 
        
  • No fake reviewer results.
    • 
        
  • Service role not used.
    • 
        
  • Browser persistence blocked.
    • 
        
  • Update/delete/approve/execute blocked.
    • 
        
  • External release controls absent.
    •
